debug_info.goal:
U V : Type
h : U ∼ V
⊢ Set U ∼ Set V

failed_attempts: h₅

by
  obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩ := h
  refine' ⟨fun s => s.image f, fun s => s.image g, _, _⟩
  · -- Prove that (image f) ∘ (image g) = id
    ext s
    simp [hgf, image_image, Function.comp]
    <;>
    aesop
  · -- Prove that (image g) ∘ (image f) = id
    ext s
    simp [hfg, image_image, Function.comp]
    <;>
    aesop

by
  have h₁ : Type U := inferInstance
  have h₂ : Type V := inferInstance
  have h₃ : Type (Set U) := inferInstance
  have h₄ : Type (Set V) := inferInstance
  -- Use the fact that the type of subsets is equinumerous if the original types are equinumerous
  have h₅ : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    -- Use the bijection between U and V to construct a bijection between their power sets
    classical
    -- Use the equivalence of U and V to get an equivalence of their power sets
    have h₆ : U ∼ V := h
    -- Use the fact that the power sets are equinumerous if the original sets are equinumerous
    have h₇ : Set U ∼ Set V := by
      -- Use the equivalence of U and V to get an equivalence of their power sets
      exact Set.equinumerous_of_bij fun s : Set U => Set.image h.toFun s
      <;> simp_all [BijOn]
      <;> (try aesop)
      <;> (try
        {
          intro s
          ext x
          simp_all [Function.Bijective]
          <;> aesop
        })
      <;> (try
        {
          intro s t hst
          simp_all [Function.Bijective]
          <;> aesop
        })
      <;> (try
        {
          intro t
          have h₈ := h.surjective
          classical
          use { x : U | h.toFun x ∈ t }
          ext x
          simp_all [Function.Bijective]
          <;> aesop
        })
    -- The result follows from the above steps
    exact h₇
  -- The final result is obtained
  exact h₅

by
  haveI : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    have h₁ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := by
      exact h
    have h₂ : Set U ∼ Set V := by
      -- Use the fact that U ≃ V to show that Set U ≃ Set V
      have h₃ : U ≃ V := by
        obtain ⟨e⟩ := h₁
        exact e
      -- Use the equivalence to map sets
      have h₄ : Set U ≃ Set V := by
        -- Use the equivalence to map sets
        exact Set.Equiv.ofBijective (Set.preimage (h₃.equivFrom : V → U)) (by
          -- Show that the preimage function is bijective
          have h₅ : Bijective (h₃.equivFrom : V → U) := by
            exact h₃.bijective
          -- Use the bijectivity to show that preimage is bijective on sets
          exact ⟨fun s t h₆ => by
            -- Injectivity
            apply Set.ext
            intro x
            simp_all [Set.mem_preimage, Function.Bijective.injective]
            <;> aesop,
            fun s => by
              -- Surjectivity
              have h₆ : Set.Nonempty (Set.univ : Set V) := by
                exact Set.univ_nonempty
              have h₇ : Set.Nonempty (s : Set U) := by
                -- If s is empty, then the preimage of an empty set is empty
                by_cases h₈ : s = ∅
                · exfalso
                  simp_all [Set.ext_iff]
                  <;> aesop
                · exact Set.nonempty_iff_ne_empty.mpr h₈
              -- Use the surjectivity of the equivalence to find a preimage
              have h₈ : ∃ (t : Set V), Set.preimage (h₃.equivFrom : V → U) t = s := by
                -- Use the fact that the equivalence is surjective
                classical
                use s.bijective.preimage (h₃.equivFrom)
                <;> simp_all [Set.ext_iff, Set.mem_preimage]
                <;> aesop
              obtain ⟨t, h₉⟩ := h₈
              exact ⟨t, by simp_all [Set.ext_iff, Set.mem_preimage]⟩⟩)
      -- Use the equivalence to show that Set U ≃ Set V
      exact h₄
    exact h₂
  exact inferInstance

by
  have h₁ : U ≃ V := by
    exact Classical.choice h
  
  have h₂ : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    refine' ⟨Equiv.SetMap h₁⟩
  
  exact h₂
Set.equiv_sets_of_bijection h

by
    -- Use the bijection between U and V to construct a bijection between Set U and Set V
    obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩ := Cardinal.instEquivExt h
    have h1 : (Set U : Type) ∼ Set V := by
      -- Define the bijection between Set U and Set V using the bijection between U and V
      refine' ⟨fun S => { v : V | ∃ u ∈ S, f u = v }, fun T => { u : U | ∃ v ∈ T, g v = u }, _, _⟩
      -- Prove that the first function is the inverse of the second
      · intro T
        ext u
        simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        constructor
        · intro hu
          -- If u is in the preimage of T, then there exists v in T such that g v = u
          obtain ⟨v, hv, rfl⟩ := hu
          refine' ⟨v, hv, _⟩
          rw [← hgf]
          <;> simp
        · intro hu
          -- If there exists v in T such that g v = u, then u is in the preimage of T
          obtain ⟨v, hv, rfl⟩ := hu
          have h2 : f (g v) = v := by
            have h3 := congr_fun hgf v
            simp at h3 ⊢
            <;> aesop
          refine' ⟨v, hv, _⟩
          <;> simp_all [h2]
          <;> aesop
      -- Prove that the second function is the inverse of the first
      · intro S
        ext v
        simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        constructor
        · intro hv
          -- If v is in the image of S, then there exists u in S such that f u = v
          obtain ⟨u, hu, rfl⟩ := hv
          refine' ⟨u, hu, _⟩
          <;> simp [hfg]
          <;> aesop
        · intro hv
          -- If there exists u in S such that f u = v, then v is in the image of S
          obtain ⟨u, hu, rfl⟩ := hv
          have h2 : g (f u) = u := by
            have h3 := congr_fun hfg u
            simp at h3 ⊢
            <;> aesop
          refine' ⟨u, hu, _⟩
          <;> simp_all [h2]
          <;> aesop
    -- Use the constructed bijection to conclude the proof
    exact h1

by
  have h₁ : U ≃ V := h
  have h₂ : Set U ≃ Set V := by
    -- Use the fact that the power set of U is equivalent to the power set of V if U and V are equivalent.
    exact (Equiv.Pi (fun _ => _) (fun _ => h₁.symm))
  -- Since Set U and Set V are equivalent, we can conclude that Set U ∼ Set V.
  exact h₂

by
  have h_main : Set U ∼ Set V := h.set
  exact h_main

by
  have h1 : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := ⟨h⟩
  exact SetEquiv.ofTypeEquiv h

by
  have h₁ : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    -- Extract the equivalence from the hypothesis
    obtain ⟨e⟩ := h
    -- Use the equivalence of sets to construct the equivalence of their power sets
    let e' : Set U ≃ Set V := e.image
    -- The image of the equivalence is itself an equivalence
    exact e'
  -- The intermediate step directly gives the desired result
  exact h₁

by
  have h1 : U ∼ V := h
  have h2 : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    -- Use the fact that the power sets of equivalent types are equivalent
    have h3 : Type U ≃ Type V := by
      -- Use the equivalence between U and V to create an equivalence between Type U and Type V
      exact Equiv.ofBijective (h) ⟨fun a b h => by simpa using h, fun b => ⟨h.symm b, fun a => by simp⟩⟩
    -- Use the equivalence between Type U and Type V to show that Set U and Set V are equivalent
    exact by
      simpa [Set.ext_iff] using (Set.equivSetOfEquiv h).symm
  exact h2

by
  have h1 : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    exact h.pow 2
  exact h1

by
  have h_main : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    -- Extract the bijection and its inverse from the given equivalence
    rcases h with ⟨f, g, h₁, h₂⟩
    -- Define the forward and backward mappings for subsets
    have h₃ : Set U ∼ Set V := by
      -- Use the bijection to map subsets of U to subsets of V and vice versa
      apply Equiv.setEquiv
      <;> simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse, Function.Bijective]
      <;>
      (try
        {
          intros
          <;>
          (try
            {
              ext x
              <;>
              simp_all [Function.LeftInverse, Function.RightInverse]
              <;>
              aesop
            })
          <;>
          aesop
        })
      <;>
      aesop
    -- Use the constructed equivalence
    exact h₃
  -- Use the main result to complete the proof
  exact h_main

by
  -- We use the fact that an isomorphism between U and V can be extended to an isomorphism between their powersets.
  -- Specifically, we define a function that maps each subset of U to its image in V under the isomorphism.
  -- We then show that this function is itself an isomorphism between the powersets.
  have h₁ : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    -- Use the isomorphism h to create a function between the powersets
    classical
    -- Use the base equivalence to construct an equivalence between the powersets
    refine' ⟨fun s => (s : Set U).image h, _⟩
    -- We need to show that this function is invertible and the inverse is also a function between powersets
    refine' ⟨fun s => (s : Set V).image h.symm, _⟩
    -- Prove that the function and its inverse are inverses of each other
    constructor
    · -- Show that applying the function and then its inverse gives the original set
      intro s
      ext x
      simp [Set.mem_image, h.symm_apply_apply, Function.comp_apply]
      <;>
      tauto
    · -- Show that applying the inverse and then the function gives the original set
      intro s
      ext x
      simp [Set.mem_image, h.apply_symm_apply, Function.comp_apply]
      <;>
      tauto
  -- Return the established isomorphism between the powersets
  exact h₁
h.setEquiv

by
  have h₁ : Set U ≃ Set V := by sorry
  exact h₁.equiv

by
  have h₁ : U ≃ V := h
  have h₂ : Set U ≃ Set V := by
    apply Equiv.setOfEquiv
    exact h₁
  exact h₂

by
  classical
  -- Extract the bijection from the equivalence
  let ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
  -- Define the function to map sets from U to V
  let F : Set U → Set V := fun s => { v : V | ∃ (u : U), f u = v ∧ u ∈ s }
  -- Define the inverse function to map sets from V to U
  let G : Set V → Set U := fun t => { u : U | ∃ (v : V), (f⁻¹) v = u ∧ v ∈ t }
  -- Prove that F is bijective by showing it has a left and right inverse
  have h₁ : Function.Injective F := by
    intro s t hst
    have h₂ : F s = F t := hst
    have h₃ : ∀ (v : V), v ∈ F s ↔ v ∈ F t := by
      intro v
      rw [h₂]
    have h₄ : ∀ (v : V), (∃ (u : U), f u = v ∧ u ∈ s) ↔ (∃ (u : U), f u = v ∧ u ∈ t) := by
      intro v
      simpa [F] using h₃ v
    have h₅ : s = t := by
      apply Set.ext
      intro u
      have h₆ : u ∈ s ↔ u ∈ t := by
        constructor
        · intro hu
          have h₇ : f u ∈ F s := by
            simp only [F]
            exact ⟨u, rfl, hu⟩
          have h₈ : f u ∈ F t := by
            rw [h₃] at h₇
            exact h₇
          have h₉ : ∃ (u' : U), f u' = f u ∧ u' ∈ t := by
            simpa [F] using h₈
          obtain ⟨u', hu'₁, hu'₂⟩ := h₉
          have h₁₀ : u' = u := by
            have h₁₁ : f u' = f u := hu'₁
            have h₁₂ : Function.Injective f := by
              exact hf.1
            exact h₁₂ h₁₁
          rw [h₁₀] at hu'₂
          exact hu'₂
        · intro hu
          have h₇ : f u ∈ F t := by
            simp only [F]
            exact ⟨u, rfl, hu⟩
          have h₈ : f u ∈ F s := by
            rw [h₃] at h₇
            exact h₇
          have h₉ : ∃ (u' : U), f u' = f u ∧ u' ∈ s := by
            simpa [F] using h₈
          obtain ⟨u', hu'₁, hu'₂⟩ := h₉
          have h₁₀ : u' = u := by
            have h₁₁ : f u' = f u := hu'₁
            have h₁₂ : Function.Injective f := by
              exact hf.1
            exact h₁₂ h₁₁
          rw [h₁₀] at hu'₂
          exact hu'₂
      exact h₆
    exact h₅
  
  have h₂ : Function.Surjective F := by
    intro t
    have h₃ : ∃ (s : Set U), F s = t := by
      -- Define s as the preimage of t under f
      use { u : U | ∃ (v : V), (f⁻¹) v = u ∧ v ∈ t }
      apply Set.ext
      intro v
      simp only [F, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if v ∈ F s, then v ∈ t
        intro h₄
        obtain ⟨u, h₅, h₆⟩ := h₄
        have h₇ : ∃ (v' : V), (f⁻¹) v' = u ∧ v' ∈ t := by
          exact ⟨f u, by
            have h₈ : f⁻¹ (f u) = u := by
              have h₉ : Function.Bijective f := by
                exact hf
              have h₁₀ : Function.Surjective f := h₉.2
              have h₁₁ : Function.Injective f := h₉.1
              simp [Function.leftInverse_invFun, h₁₁]
            rw [h₈]
            <;> simp_all [hf.1]
          , by
            have h₈ : f u = v := by simp_all
            have h₉ : u ∈ { u : U | ∃ (v : V), (f⁻¹) v = u ∧ v ∈ t } := by simp_all
            obtain ⟨v', h₁₀, h₁₁⟩ := h₉
            have h₁₂ : f u ∈ t := by
              simp_all [hf.1]
              <;>
              (try { aesop }) <;>
              (try { simp_all [Function.Bijective] }) <;>
              (try { aesop })
            simp_all
          ⟩
        obtain ⟨v', h₇, h₈⟩ := h₇
        have h₉ : f u = v := by simp_all
        have h₁₀ : v' ∈ t := by simp_all
        have h₁₁ : f u = v := by simp_all
        have h₁₂ : v ∈ t := by
          have h₁₃ : f u = v := by simp_all
          have h₁₄ : f u ∈ t := by
            simp_all [hf.1]
            <;>
            (try { aesop }) <;>
            (try { simp_all [Function.Bijective] }) <;>
            (try { aesop })
          simp_all
        simp_all
      · -- Prove the reverse direction: if v ∈ t, then v ∈ F s
        intro h₄
        have h₅ : v ∈ t := h₄
        have h₆ : ∃ (u : U), f u = v := by
          have h₇ : Function.Surjective f := hf.2
          exact h₇ v
        obtain ⟨u, h₇⟩ := h₆
        refine' ⟨u, _⟩
        constructor
        · -- Prove f u = v
          simp_all
        · -- Prove u ∈ s
          have h₈ : u ∈ { u : U | ∃ (v : V), (f⁻¹) v = u ∧ v ∈ t } := by
            refine' ⟨v, _⟩
            constructor
            · -- Prove f⁻¹ v = u
              have h₉ : f⁻¹ v = u := by
                have h₁₀ : Function.Bijective f := hf
                have h₁₁ : Function.Injective f := h₁₀.1
                have h₁₂ : Function.Surjective f := h₁₀.2
                have h₁₃ : f u = v := by simp_all
                have h₁₄ : f⁻¹ v = u := by
                  apply Eq.symm
                  apply Eq.symm
                  have h₁₅ : f u = v := by simp_all
                  have h₁₆ : f⁻¹ v = u := by
                    rw [← h₁₅]
                    simp [Function.leftInverse_invFun, h₁₁]
                  exact h₁₆
                exact h₁₄
              simp_all
            · -- Prove v ∈ t
              simp_all
          simp_all
    -- Obtain the set s such that F s = t
    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := h₃
    -- Show that s is the preimage of t under F
    exact ⟨s, hs⟩
  
  -- Use the bijection to show the equivalence of the power sets
  exact Equiv.SetOfEquiv h

by
  classical
  have h2 : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := by
    -- Use the given equivalence to find an equivalence between U and V
    have h3 : U ∼ V := h
    -- Use the classical choice principle to get an equivalence
    exact ⟨h3.choose⟩
  -- Use the equivalence to construct an equivalence between Set U and Set V
  cases' h2 with e
  have h4 : Set U ≃ Set V := by
    -- Use the equivalence to construct an equivalence between Set U and Set V
    exact e.set
  -- Use the equivalence to show that Set U and Set V are in bijection
  exact ⟨h4⟩

by
  have h₁ : Set U ∼ Set V := Set.equinum_of_equipotent h
  exact h₁

by
  have h1 : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    have h2 : U ∼ V := h
    have h3 : Set U ∼ Set V := by
      -- Use the fact that if U ∼ V, then their power sets are also equinumerous
      classical
      exact Set.equinumerous_iff_injective_forall_surjective.mpr ⟨fun s : Set U => Set.image (h.choose) s, fun s t hst => by
        -- Prove that the image function is injective
        have h4 : Set.image (h.choose) s = Set.image (h.choose) t := hst
        have h5 : Set.image (h.choose) s = Set.image (h.choose) t := h4
        have h6 : s = t := by
          apply Set.ext
          intro x
          have h7 : x ∈ s ↔ x ∈ t := by
            constructor
            · intro hx
              have h8 : (h.choose) x ∈ Set.image (h.choose) s := by
                exact Set.mem_image_of_mem (h.choose) hx
              have h9 : (h.choose) x ∈ Set.image (h.choose) t := by
                rw [h5] at h8
                exact h8
              have h10 : ∃ (y : V), (h.choose) x = y ∧ y ∈ t := by
                simpa [Set.mem_image] using h9
              rcases h10 with ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩
              have h11 : y = (h.choose) x := by rw [hy1]
              have h12 : x ∈ t := by
                have h13 : Function.Bijective (h.choose) := by
                  exact h.choose_spec.bijective
                have h14 : Function.LeftInverse (h.choose⁻¹) (h.choose) := by
                  exact h.choose_spec.leftInverse
                have h15 : Function.RightInverse (h.choose⁻¹) (h.choose) := by
                  exact h.choose_spec.rightInverse
                have h16 : y ∈ t := hy2
                have h17 : (h.choose⁻¹) y = x := by
                  have h18 : (h.choose⁻¹) ((h.choose) x) = x := by
                    apply Function.leftInverse_invFun
                    exact h.choose_spec.bijective.1
                  have h19 : y = (h.choose) x := by rw [hy1]
                  rw [h19]
                  exact h18
                have h20 : (h.choose⁻¹) y ∈ (h.choose⁻¹) '' t := by
                  exact Set.mem_image_of_mem (h.choose⁻¹) hy2
                have h21 : (h.choose⁻¹) y = x := h17
                rw [h21] at h20
                exact h20
              exact h12
            · intro hx
              have h8 : (h.choose) x ∈ Set.image (h.choose) t := by
                exact Set.mem_image_of_mem (h.choose) hx
              have h9 : (h.choose) x ∈ Set.image (h.choose) s := by
                rw [h5] at *
                exact h8
              have h10 : ∃ (y : V), (h.choose) x = y ∧ y ∈ s := by
                simpa [Set.mem_image] using h9
              rcases h10 with ⟨y, hy1, hy2⟩
              have h11 : y = (h.choose) x := by rw [hy1]
              have h12 : x ∈ s := by
                have h13 : Function.Bijective (h.choose) := by
                  exact h.choose_spec.bijective
                have h14 : Function.LeftInverse (h.choose⁻¹) (h.choose) := by
                  exact h.choose_spec.leftInverse
                have h15 : Function.RightInverse (h.choose⁻¹) (h.choose) := by
                  exact h.choose_spec.rightInverse
                have h16 : y ∈ s := hy2
                have h17 : (h.choose⁻¹) y = x := by
                  have h18 : (h.choose⁻¹) ((h.choose) x) = x := by
                    apply Function.leftInverse_invFun
                    exact h.choose_spec.bijective.1
                  have h19 : y = (h.choose) x := by rw [hy1]
                  rw [h19]
                  exact h18
                have h20 : (h.choose⁻¹) y ∈ (h.choose⁻¹) '' s := by
                  exact Set.mem_image_of_mem (h.choose⁻¹) hy2
                have h21 : (h.choose⁻¹) y = x := h17
                rw [h21] at h20
                exact h20
              exact h12
          exact h7
        exact h6
      , fun t : Set V => by
        -- Prove that the image function is surjective
        have h4 : Function.Bijective (h.choose) := by
          exact h.choose_spec.bijective
        have h5 : Function.LeftInverse (h.choose⁻¹) (h.choose) := by
          exact h.choose_spec.leftInverse
        have h6 : Function.RightInverse (h.choose⁻¹) (h.choose) := by
          exact h.choose_spec.rightInverse
        have h7 : ∃ (s : Set U), Set.image (h.choose) s = t := by
          use (h.choose⁻¹) '' t
          apply Set.ext
          intro y
          have h8 : y ∈ Set.image (h.choose) ((h.choose⁻¹) '' t) ↔ y ∈ t := by
            constructor
            · intro hy
              rcases hy with ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
              have h9 : x ∈ (h.choose⁻¹) '' t := hx
              have h10 : ∃ (z : V), z ∈ t ∧ (h.choose⁻¹) z = x := by
                simpa [Set.mem_image] using h9
              rcases h10 with ⟨z, hz1, hz2⟩
              have h11 : (h.choose) x = z := by
                have h12 : (h.choose) (h.choose⁻¹ z) = z := by
                  apply Function.rightInverse_invFun
                  exact h.choose_spec.bijective.2
                have h13 : x = (h.choose⁻¹) z := by
                  apply Eq.symm
                  apply Eq.symm
                  rw [hz2]
                rw [h13]
                <;> rw [h12]
              have h14 : z ∈ t := hz1
              have h15 : (h.choose) x = z := h11
              have h16 : (h.choose) x = y := by
                simp_all
              rw [h16] at *
              exact h14
            · intro hy
              have h9 : y ∈ t := hy
              have h10 : (h.choose) ((h.choose⁻¹) y) = y := by
                apply Function.rightInverse_invFun
                exact h.choose_spec.bijective.2
              have h11 : (h.choose⁻¹) y ∈ (h.choose⁻¹) '' t := by
                exact Set.mem_image_of_mem (h.choose⁻¹) hy
              refine' ⟨(h.choose⁻¹) y, h11, _⟩
              <;> simp_all
          simp_all
        rcases h7 with ⟨s, hs⟩
        exact ⟨s, by simp_all⟩⟩
    exact h3
  exact h1

by
  have h_main : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hgf⟩ := h
    refine' ⟨fun s => {v | ∃ u ∈ s, f u = v}, fun s => {u | ∃ v ∈ s, g v = u}, _, _⟩
    · -- Prove that the composition of the two functions is the identity on Set V
      intro s
      ext v
      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      constructor
      · -- Forward inclusion
        intro hv
        obtain ⟨u, hu, hv'⟩ := hv
        have h₁ : g (f u) = u := by
          rw [← hgf]
          <;> simp
        have h₂ : g (f u) = u := by
          rw [← hgf]
          <;> simp
        have h₃ : ∃ v', v' ∈ s ∧ g v' = v := by
          refine' ⟨f u, hu, _⟩
          simp_all [h₁]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₃
      · -- Backward inclusion
        intro hv
        obtain ⟨v', hv', hv''⟩ := hv
        have h₁ : f (g v') = v' := by
          rw [← hfg]
          <;> simp
        have h₂ : ∃ u ∈ {u | ∃ v ∈ s, g v = u}, f u = v := by
          refine' ⟨g v', _, _⟩
          · -- Prove that g v' is in the set
            refine' ⟨v', hv', _⟩
            <;> simp_all
          · -- Prove that f (g v') = v
            simp_all [h₁]
            <;> aesop
        exact h₂
    · -- Prove that the composition of the two functions is the identity on Set U
      intro s
      ext u
      simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      constructor
      · -- Forward inclusion
        intro hu
        obtain ⟨v, hv, hu'⟩ := hu
        have h₁ : f (g v) = v := by
          rw [← hfg]
          <;> simp
        have h₂ : ∃ u', u' ∈ s ∧ f u' = u := by
          refine' ⟨g v, _, _⟩
          · -- Prove that g v is in the set
            refine' ⟨v, hv, _⟩
            <;> simp_all
          · -- Prove that f (g v) = u
            simp_all [h₁]
            <;> aesop
        exact h₂
      · -- Backward inclusion
        intro hu
        obtain ⟨u', hu', hu''⟩ := hu
        have h₁ : g (f u') = u' := by
          rw [← hgf]
          <;> simp
        have h₂ : ∃ v, v ∈ s ∧ g v = u := by
          refine' ⟨f u', hu', _⟩
          simp_all [h₁]
          <;> aesop
        exact h₂
  exact h_main

by
  have h₁ : U ∼ V := h
  have h₂ : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    have h₃ : {s : Set U} ∼ {s : Set V} := by
      -- Use the equivalence between the types to define an equivalence between their powersets.
      have h₄ : {s : Set U} ∼ {s : Set V} := by
        -- Define the forward and backward functions using the equivalence between U and V.
        classical
        have h₅ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := by
          obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
          refine' ⟨f⟩
        -- Use the equivalence to construct the equivalence between the powersets.
        have h₆ : {s : Set U} ∼ {s : Set V} := by
          -- Use the fact that the cardinality of the powerset is 2^cardinality of the base set.
          classical
          obtain ⟨f⟩ := h₅
          let g : {s : Set U} → {s : Set V} := fun s => ⟨f '' s.val, by
            -- Show that the image of a set under the equivalence is a set in V.
            exact Set.image_mem _ s.val.prop
            ⟩
          let h₁ : {s : Set V} → {s : Set U} := fun s => ⟨f.symm '' s.val, by
            -- Show that the preimage of a set under the equivalence is a set in U.
            exact Set.image_mem _ s.val.prop
            ⟩
          -- Show that the constructed functions are inverses of each other.
          refine' ⟨g, h₁, _, _⟩
          -- First direction of the equivalence.
          · intro s
            simp_all [Set.ext_iff]
            <;> aesop
          -- Second direction of the equivalence.
          · intro s
            simp_all [Set.ext_iff]
            <;> aesop
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    -- Convert the equivalence between the types to the equivalence between their powersets.
    simpa [Set.ext_iff] using h₃
  exact h₂

by
  have h1 : U ∼ V := h
  have h2 : Set U ∼ Set V := by
    -- Use the equivalence between U and V to construct an equivalence between their subsets
    let f : Set U → Set V := fun s => {u : V | ∃ x : U, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ s}
    let g : Set V → Set U := fun t => {u : U | ∃ x : V, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ t}
    -- Show that f and g are inverses of each other
    have h3 : g ∘ f = id := by
      funext s
      ext x
      simp only [Function.comp_apply, f, g]
      constructor
      · -- Prove that if x ∈ g (f s), then x ∈ s
        intro hx
        rcases hx with ⟨y, ⟨z, ⟨h4, h5⟩, h6⟩, h7⟩
        have h8 : x ≈ y := by
          have h9 : y ≈ z := by
            simp_all [Equiv.symm_def]
            <;> aesop
          have h10 : x ≈ z := by
            simp_all [Equiv.symm_def]
            <;> aesop
          calc
            x ≈ z := h10
            _ ≈ y := h9.symm
        have h9 : z ∈ s := by
          aesop
        have h10 : x ∈ s := by
          have h11 : x ≈ z := by
            simp_all [Equiv.symm_def]
            <;> aesop
          have h12 : z ∈ s := h9
          simp_all [Equiv.symm_def]
          <;> aesop
        exact h10
      · -- Prove that if x ∈ s, then x ∈ g (f s)
        intro hx
        have h4 : ∃ (y : V), y ≈ x ∧ y ∈ ( {u : V | ∃ x : U, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ s} : Set V) := by
          -- Use the fact that U and V are equivalent to find a corresponding element in V
          have h5 : Nonempty V := by
            have h6 : Nonempty U := by
              by_contra h7
              have h8 : U ∼ V := h1
              have h9 : Nonempty U := by
                exfalso
                simp_all [Nonempty]
              contradiction
            have h10 : Nonempty U := h6
            have h11 : Nonempty V := by
              have h12 : Nonempty U := h10
              have h13 : U ∼ V := h1
              have h14 : Nonempty V := by
                have h15 : Nonempty U := h12
                exact h13.nonempty_iff.mpr h15
              exact h14
            exact h11
          -- Choose an element in V and use the equivalence to find a corresponding element in U
          cases' h5 with y
          have h6 : y ≈ y := by simp
          have h7 : ∃ (x : U), x ≈ y := by
            have h8 : U ∼ V := h1
            have h9 : Nonempty U := by
              have h10 : Nonempty V := by
                exact ⟨y⟩
              exact h1.nonempty_iff.mpr h10
            cases' h9 with x
            have h10 : x ≈ (h1.toFun x : V) := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
            have h11 : (h1.symm.toFun y : U) ≈ y := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
            exact ⟨h1.symm.toFun y, by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]⟩
          cases' h7 with x h8
          use y
          constructor
          · simp [h8]
          · exact ⟨x, by simp_all [Equiv.apply_symm_apply], hx⟩
        -- Use the existence of the element to show that x ∈ g (f s)
        rcases h4 with ⟨y, h5, h6⟩
        have h7 : ∃ (z : U), z ≈ y ∧ z ∈ ({u : U | ∃ x : V, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ ({u : V | ∃ x : U, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ s} : Set V)} : Set U) := by
          have h8 : ∃ (z : U), z ≈ y := by
            have h9 : U ∼ V := h1
            have h10 : Nonempty U := by
              have h11 : Nonempty V := by
                exact ⟨y⟩
              exact h1.nonempty_iff.mpr h11
            cases' h10 with z
            have h11 : z ≈ (h1.toFun z : V) := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
            have h12 : (h1.symm.toFun y : U) ≈ y := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
            exact ⟨h1.symm.toFun y, by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]⟩
          cases' h8 with z h9
          refine' ⟨z, h9, _⟩
          have h10 : y ∈ ({u : V | ∃ x : U, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ s} : Set V) := h6
          have h11 : ∃ (x : U), x ≈ y ∧ x ∈ s := by
            simpa using h10
          obtain ⟨x, h12, h13⟩ := h11
          refine' ⟨y, _⟩
          <;> aesop
        rcases h7 with ⟨z, h8, h9⟩
        refine' ⟨z, _⟩
        <;> aesop
    have h4 : f ∘ g = id := by
      funext t
      ext x
      simp only [Function.comp_apply, f, g]
      constructor
      · -- Prove that if x ∈ f (g t), then x ∈ t
        intro hx
        rcases hx with ⟨y, ⟨z, ⟨h5, h6⟩, h7⟩, h8⟩
        have h9 : y ≈ x := by
          have h10 : z ≈ y := by
            simp_all [Equiv.symm_def]
            <;> aesop
          have h11 : z ≈ x := by
            simp_all [Equiv.symm_def]
            <;> aesop
          calc
            y ≈ z := h10.symm
            _ ≈ x := h11
        have h10 : z ∈ t := by
          aesop
        have h11 : x ∈ t := by
          have h12 : x ≈ z := by
            simp_all [Equiv.symm_def]
            <;> aesop
          have h13 : z ∈ t := h10
          simp_all [Equiv.symm_def]
          <;> aesop
        exact h11
      · -- Prove that if x ∈ t, then x ∈ f (g t)
        intro hx
        have h5 : ∃ (y : U), y ≈ x ∧ y ∈ ({u : U | ∃ x : V, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ t} : Set U) := by
          -- Use the fact that U and V are equivalent to find a corresponding element in U
          have h6 : Nonempty U := by
            have h7 : Nonempty V := by
              by_contra h8
              have h9 : U ∼ V := h1
              have h10 : Nonempty V := by
                exfalso
                simp_all [Nonempty]
              contradiction
            have h8 : Nonempty V := h7
            have h9 : Nonempty U := by
              have h10 : Nonempty V := h8
              have h11 : U ∼ V := h1
              have h12 : Nonempty U := by
                have h13 : Nonempty V := h10
                exact h1.nonempty_iff.mpr h13
              exact h12
            exact h9
          -- Choose an element in U and use the equivalence to find a corresponding element in V
          cases' h6 with y
          have h7 : y ≈ y := by simp
          have h8 : ∃ (x : V), x ≈ y ∧ x ∈ t := by
            have h9 : U ∼ V := h1
            have h10 : Nonempty V := by
              have h11 : Nonempty U := by
                exact ⟨y⟩
              exact h1.nonempty_iff.mpr h11
            cases' h10 with x
            have h11 : (h1.symm.toFun x : U) ≈ x := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
            have h12 : y ≈ (h1.toFun y : V) := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
            have h13 : x ∈ t → ∃ (x : V), x ≈ y ∧ x ∈ t := by
              intro h14
              exact ⟨x, by
                have h15 : (h1.symm.toFun x : U) ≈ x := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
                have h16 : y ≈ (h1.toFun y : V) := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
                simp_all [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
                <;> aesop, h14⟩
            by_cases h14 : x ∈ t
            · exact h13 h14
            · exfalso
              simp_all [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
              <;> aesop
          cases' h8 with x h9
          use y
          constructor
          · simp_all [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
            <;> aesop
          · exact ⟨x, by simp_all [Equiv.apply_symm_apply], by simp_all [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]⟩
        -- Use the existence of the element to show that x ∈ f (g t)
        rcases h5 with ⟨y, h6, h7⟩
        have h8 : ∃ (z : V), z ≈ y ∧ z ∈ ({u : V | ∃ x : U, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ t} : Set V) := by
          have h9 : ∃ (z : V), z ≈ y := by
            have h10 : U ∼ V := h1
            have h11 : Nonempty V := by
              have h12 : Nonempty U := by
                exact ⟨y⟩
              exact h1.nonempty_iff.mpr h12
            cases' h11 with z
            have h12 : (h1.toFun y : V) ≈ y := by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
            have h13 : z ≈ z := by simp
            have h14 : ∃ (z : V), z ≈ y := by
              exact ⟨(h1.toFun y : V), by simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]⟩
            exact h14
          cases' h9 with z h10
          refine' ⟨z, h10, _⟩
          have h11 : y ∈ ({u : U | ∃ x : V, x ≈ u ∧ x ∈ t} : Set U) := h7
          have h12 : ∃ (x : V), x ≈ y ∧ x ∈ t := by
            simpa using h11
          obtain ⟨x, h13, h14⟩ := h12
          refine' ⟨y, _⟩
          <;> aesop
        rcases h8 with ⟨z, h9, h10⟩
        refine' ⟨z, _⟩
        <;> aesop
    -- Construct the equivalence between Set U and Set V
    exact ⟨f, g, h4, h3⟩
  exact h2
⟨Set.equivSetOfEquiv (Classical.choose h)⟩
Set.Equiv.ofEquiv h

by
  -- Step 1: Extract the equivalence U ≃ V from U ∼ V
  have h₁ : U ≃ V := by sorry

  -- Step 2: Lift the equivalence to sets to get Set U ≃ Set V
  have h₂ : Set U ≃ Set V := by sorry

  -- Step 3: Show that Set U ≃ Set V implies Set U ∼ Set V
  have h₃ : Set U ∼ Set V := by sorry

  -- Final step: Use the above to conclude
  exact h₃
sorry
